enrichment.short_description: Open-source groupware suite combining email, calendar, CRM, file sharing, and team chat for self-hosted deployment.

long_form_blocks.heading: Overview
paragraphs: EGroupware is an open-source collaboration platform built around email, calendar, contacts, file management, and CRM. It positions itself as an alternative to hosted office suites for organizations that want to keep their data on their own servers.

The Community Edition is free and self-hosted, while EGroupware GmbH, the German company behind the project, also sells a paid Enterprise Public License (EPL) edition and cloud-hosted plans that add support and extra modules such as e-invoicing (XRechnung/ZUGFeRD) and hybrid full-text/RAG search.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who It's For
paragraphs: EGroupware targets SMEs, government agencies, and educational institutions that need an integrated communication and project suite while keeping infrastructure under their own control, particularly in markets where data sovereignty is a priority.

features.title: Calendar & scheduling
features.description: Appointment management with reminders and resource booking for meeting rooms and equipment.

features.title: Integrated email client
features.description: Webmail linked directly to contacts, calendar entries, and shared files.

features.title: Contact management & CRM
features.description: Shared address books and customer relationship tracking for sales and support teams.

features.title: File management
features.description: Secure document storage with granular, per-user and per-group permissions.

features.title: Chat & video conferencing
features.description: Team messaging plus video calls via Jitsi or BigBlueButton integration.

features.title: Collaborative document editing
features.description: Real-time co-editing of office documents through a Collabora Online integration.

features.title: Kanban boards & helpdesk
features.description: Project boards and a ticketing module for support and task workflows.

features.title: AI-assisted search and text tools
features.description: Hybrid full-text and RAG search plus AI helpers for email and text fields, introduced in newer versions.

pros: Free, self-hosted Community Edition with full data control
Broad all-in-one feature set covering email, CRM, files, and chat
German-based vendor emphasizing data sovereignty
Mobile apps available alongside the web interface
cons: Advanced support and some modules require the paid EPL edition or a cloud plan
Self-hosting requires ongoing server administration
Newer AI and e-invoicing features are still maturing

faqs.question: Is EGroupware free?
faqs.answer: The Community Edition is free and open source for self-hosting. A paid Enterprise Public License edition and hosted cloud plans add support and extra features.

faqs.question: Can EGroupware be used on mobile devices?
faqs.answer: Yes, it offers a responsive web interface along with mobile apps for smartphones and tablets.

faqs.question: Who develops EGroupware?
faqs.answer: EGroupware GmbH, a Germany-based company, develops, hosts, and supports the platform.

faqs.question: Does EGroupware include AI features?
faqs.answer: Recent versions add AI-assisted hybrid search and AI tools for composing email and text content.

use_cases.title: Self-hosted office replacement
use_cases.description: Organizations replacing Microsoft 365 or Google Workspace with an on-premises suite.

use_cases.title: Data-sovereign collaboration
use_cases.description: Teams that must keep email, files, and CRM data within their own or EU infrastructure.

enrichment.seo_meta_description: EGroupware is an open-source groupware suite with email, calendar, CRM, and file sharing, self-hosted for full data control.

enrichment.short_description: Open-source groupware and CRM platform for email, calendar, files, and business collaboration, self-hosted or hosted online.

long_form_blocks.heading: Overview
paragraphs: Group-Office is an open-source groupware and CRM platform from Intermesh BV, a company based in 's-Hertogenbosch, Netherlands. It bundles email, calendar, contacts, CRM, file sharing, and project management into a single web interface.

It syncs with desktop and mobile devices over open protocols (IMAP, Microsoft ActiveSync, CalDAV, CardDAV, and WebDAV), and its Studio feature lets administrators build custom modules without external development tools.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who It's For
paragraphs: The platform suits privacy-focused businesses that want on-premises or European-hosted collaboration and CRM, with the option to extend the system for their own workflows via Studio.

features.title: Document co-editing
features.description: Real-time collaborative editing alongside meeting scheduling and chat.

features.title: CRM & email marketing
features.description: Contact management, sales pipeline, and email marketing tools.

features.title: Project management
features.description: Asset storage, time tracking, cost monitoring, and progress tracking per project.

features.title: File management
features.description: Secure storage, sharing, and template-based collaborative editing.

features.title: Studio module builder
features.description: Create custom business apps and modules directly inside Group-Office.

features.title: Cross-device sync
features.description: IMAP, ActiveSync, CalDAV, CardDAV, and WebDAV support for iOS, macOS, Android, and Windows.

pros: Free, self-hosted Community Edition
Open protocol sync across iOS, macOS, Android, and Windows
Combines groupware and CRM in one product
Extendable through the built-in Studio module builder
cons: Hosted Online Edition pricing is not published on the website
Self-hosting requires server setup and maintenance
Smaller ecosystem and community than major SaaS collaboration suites

faqs.question: Is Group-Office free?
faqs.answer: The Community Edition is free and open source for self-hosting. A hosted Online Edition with a free trial is also available.

faqs.question: Does Group-Office sync with mobile devices?
faqs.answer: Yes, via open protocols including ActiveSync, CalDAV, CardDAV, and WebDAV on iOS, macOS, Android, and Windows.

faqs.question: Who makes Group-Office?
faqs.answer: Intermesh BV, a company headquartered in 's-Hertogenbosch, Netherlands.

faqs.question: Can I build custom features in Group-Office?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Studio feature lets administrators create custom modules within the platform.

use_cases.title: Self-hosted groupware + CRM
use_cases.description: SMBs wanting one open-source system for email, calendar, and customer management.

use_cases.title: Custom business apps
use_cases.description: Teams building bespoke workflow tools on top of Group-Office using Studio.

enrichment.seo_meta_description: Group-Office is an open-source groupware and CRM platform for email, calendar, files, and projects, self-hosted or hosted online.

enrichment.short_description: Free, open-source video conferencing platform with whiteboard, screen sharing, and recording, developed by the Apache Software Foundation.

long_form_blocks.heading: Overview
paragraphs: Apache OpenMeetings is an open-source video conferencing and collaboration server released under the Apache License. It uses the Kurento media server for streaming and provides browser-based audio/video calls, a multi-instance whiteboard, screen sharing with MP4 recording, and file management with private and public drives.
As an Apache Software Foundation project, it is entirely community-maintained and free to self-host, with no vendor-controlled paid tier.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who It's For
paragraphs: OpenMeetings suits organizations and developers who want a self-hosted video conferencing and webinar server they can integrate into their own infrastructure, such as educational platforms or internal collaboration tools.

features.title: Video/audio conferencing
features.description: Audio+video, audio-only, video-only, or picture-only modes with adjustable quality.

features.title: Interactive whiteboard
features.description: Multiple whiteboard instances supporting drawing and import of PDF, DOC, ODT, and PPT files.

features.title: Screen sharing & recording
features.description: Record sessions and download them as MP4 files.

features.title: File explorer
features.description: Drag-and-drop file management with private and public drive areas.

features.title: Calendar & invitations
features.description: Schedule meetings and send secure invitation links.

features.title: Polls & moderation
features.description: Create polls/surveys and manage granular moderator permissions.

pros: Completely free under the Apache License
Self-hosted for full control over meeting data
Combines video, whiteboard, recording, and file sharing in one server
Runs in the browser via HTML5, no plugin required
cons: Requires self-hosting and Kurento media server configuration
No official native mobile apps (browser-based access only)
Smaller community and slower release cadence than commercial video platforms

faqs.question: Is Apache OpenMeetings free?
faqs.answer: Yes, it is fully open source and free under the Apache License with no paid tier.

faqs.question: Who maintains OpenMeetings?
faqs.answer: It is a project of the Apache Software Foundation, maintained by its open-source community.

faqs.question: Can I self-host OpenMeetings?
faqs.answer: Yes, it is designed to be deployed on your own servers.

faqs.question: Does OpenMeetings support recording?
faqs.answer: Yes, sessions can be recorded and downloaded as MP4 files.

use_cases.title: Self-hosted webinars and classes
use_cases.description: Educational institutions running online lessons without a commercial video vendor.

use_cases.title: Internal video conferencing
use_cases.description: Organizations that need meeting infrastructure they fully control and host.

enrichment.seo_meta_description: Apache OpenMeetings is a free, open-source, self-hosted video conferencing platform with whiteboard, recording, and file sharing.

enrichment.short_description: Open-source groupware server providing webmail, calendar, and contacts with CalDAV, CardDAV, and ActiveSync device sync.

long_form_blocks.heading: Overview
paragraphs: SOGo is an open-source groupware server that provides an AJAX-based webmail, calendar, and address book interface, built around open standards rather than a proprietary protocol. It supports CalDAV, CardDAV, GroupDAV, and Microsoft ActiveSync, so it syncs natively with iPhone, Android, Windows Phone, and desktop clients, including Outlook through a third-party connector.

The software is released under the GNU GPL/LGPL and is maintained by its community together with Alinto. Commercial premium support is available as a paid add-on for organizations that want dedicated assistance.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who It's For
paragraphs: SOGo fits organizations and hosting providers that want a scalable, standards-based alternative to Exchange, capable of serving thousands of concurrent mailboxes without vendor lock-in.

features.title: AJAX webmail interface
features.description: Browser-based email client for managing messages, folders, and search.

features.title: Calendar & tasks
features.description: Shared calendaring and task lists synced across devices.

features.title: Contacts / address book
features.description: Shared address books accessible via CardDAV.

features.title: Open protocol sync
features.description: CalDAV, CardDAV, GroupDAV, and Microsoft ActiveSync support for native mobile sync.

features.title: Outlook integration
features.description: Connects to Microsoft Outlook via a third-party synchronizer.

features.title: Proven at scale
features.description: Deployed in production environments handling thousands of concurrent users.

pros: Free and open source under GPL/LGPL
Standards-based sync avoids vendor lock-in
Native mobile device support without extra apps, via ActiveSync
Scales to large, multi-thousand-user deployments
cons: Interface is more utilitarian than modern SaaS webmail clients
Outlook integration requires a separate third-party connector
Commercial premium support is a paid add-on outside the free core

faqs.question: Is SOGo free?
faqs.answer: Yes, the core software is free and open source under the GNU GPL/LGPL.

faqs.question: Does SOGo work with Outlook?
faqs.answer: Yes, through a third-party synchronizer connector, alongside native CalDAV/CardDAV/ActiveSync support for other clients.

faqs.question: Can I try SOGo before installing it?
faqs.answer: Yes, a free online demo is available on the SOGo website.

faqs.question: Who supports SOGo?
faqs.answer: It is maintained by its open-source community and Alinto, which also offers paid premium support.

use_cases.title: Exchange replacement
use_cases.description: Organizations moving off Exchange to an open standards-based groupware server.

use_cases.title: Multi-device mail and calendar sync
use_cases.description: Businesses needing native calendar/contact sync across phones and desktops without extra software.

enrichment.seo_meta_description: SOGo is a free, open-source groupware server offering webmail, calendar, and contacts with CalDAV, CardDAV, and ActiveSync sync.

enrichment.short_description: German open-source groupware suite for email, calendar, CRM, and project time tracking, self-hosted or via managed cloud.

long_form_blocks.heading: Overview
paragraphs: Tine is a groupware suite developed by METAWAYS Infosystems GmbH, headquartered in Hamburg, Germany. It combines calendar, email, an address book positioned as a relationship manager, task and project management, file storage, project time tracking with billing, and a CRM module with mobile access.
The website references both a community edition and cloud/enterprise options, though specific pricing for the paid tiers is not published.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who It's For
paragraphs: Tine targets businesses and organizations, particularly in Germany and the EU, that need integrated groupware plus project time tracking and CRM with the option of data-sovereign hosting.

features.title: Calendar
features.description: Appointment management with recurring events and resource planning.

features.title: Email
features.description: Built-in email client for workplace communication.

features.title: Address book / relationship manager
features.description: Contact management framed around ongoing relationships, not just records.

features.title: Task & project management
features.description: Project-oriented task tracking in place of a simple to-do list.

features.title: File manager
features.description: Document storage and sharing with access controls.

features.title: Project time tracking & billing
features.description: Time logging tied to projects for accounting and invoicing.

features.title: CRM
features.description: Customer relationship management with mobile access.

pros: Germany-based vendor with a data-sovereignty focus
Combines groupware, CRM, and project time tracking in one suite
Self-hosted community edition available
Open-source core
cons: Cloud/enterprise pricing is not published on the website
Smaller international community than major groupware suites
Self-hosting requires server administration

faqs.question: Is Tine free?
faqs.answer: A self-hosted community edition is free; cloud and enterprise options are paid, with pricing available on request.

faqs.question: Who develops Tine?
faqs.answer: METAWAYS Infosystems GmbH, headquartered in Hamburg, Germany.

faqs.question: Does Tine include CRM?
faqs.answer: Yes, it includes a CRM module with mobile access alongside its groupware features.

faqs.question: Can Tine track project time for billing?
faqs.answer: Yes, it includes project time tracking tied to billing and accounting.

use_cases.title: Data-sovereign groupware
use_cases.description: German/EU organizations needing collaboration tools hosted under local jurisdiction.

use_cases.title: Combined CRM and time tracking
use_cases.description: Agencies that need CRM, task management, and billable time tracking in one system.

enrichment.seo_meta_description: Tine is a German open-source groupware suite for email, calendar, CRM, and project time tracking, self-hosted or cloud-hosted.

enrichment.short_description: Email and collaboration suite with webmail, calendar, file sharing, and chat, offering a free open-source edition and paid enterprise tiers.

long_form_blocks.heading: Overview
paragraphs: Zimbra Collaboration is an email and collaboration suite combining webmail, shared calendars, contacts, tasks, and file sharing (Briefcase) with team chat and in-browser document editing. Founded in 2003, it is now owned and operated by Synacor, Inc., headquartered in Buffalo, New York.

The platform is available as a free open-source community edition covering the core mail server, alongside paid Network Edition Standard and Professional tiers that add features such as high-availability clustering, S/MIME encryption, two-factor authentication, and compliance archiving. Exact pricing for the paid tiers is not published on the site.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who It's For
paragraphs: Zimbra is aimed at organizations that need on-premises, hybrid, or private-cloud email with strong compliance and security controls, including regulated industries such as finance, government, and education.

features.title: Webmail
features.description: Browser-based email with search and message organization tools.

features.title: Shared calendars
features.description: Team scheduling across time zones with shared calendar views.

features.title: Briefcase file sharing
features.description: Secure document storage and sharing with granular permissions.

features.title: Team chat
features.description: Enterprise messaging with file sharing and group collaboration.

features.title: In-browser document editing
features.description: Create and edit office documents without leaving the webmail interface.

features.title: Security controls
features.description: S/MIME encryption, two-factor authentication, and compliance archiving on paid tiers.

features.title: Mobile & desktop sync
features.description: ActiveSync support for iOS/Android plus Outlook and desktop client integration.

pros: Mature platform with a large installed base of mailboxes worldwide
Flexible deployment: on-premises, hybrid, or private cloud
Strong optional security features (S/MIME, 2FA, compliance archiving)
Free open-source core server available
cons: High-availability clustering and advanced encryption require a paid Network Edition
Pricing for paid tiers is not published and requires contacting sales
Community edition receives less formal support than licensed tiers

faqs.question: Is there a free version of Zimbra?
faqs.answer: Yes, an open-source community edition provides the core mail server for free; advanced features require a paid Network Edition.

faqs.question: Who owns Zimbra?
faqs.answer: Zimbra is owned and operated by Synacor, Inc., headquartered in Buffalo, New York.

faqs.question: Does Zimbra support mobile devices?
faqs.answer: Yes, via ActiveSync for iOS and Android as well as Outlook integration.

faqs.question: Can Zimbra be deployed on-premises?
faqs.answer: Yes, it supports on-premises, hybrid, and private-cloud deployment.

use_cases.title: Regulated-industry email
use_cases.description: Finance, government, and education organizations needing on-prem email with compliance controls.

use_cases.title: Exchange migration
use_cases.description: Organizations moving off Microsoft Exchange to a self-hosted or hybrid alternative.

enrichment.seo_meta_description: Zimbra Collaboration offers webmail, calendar, and file sharing with a free open-source edition and paid enterprise tiers.

enrichment.short_description: Self-hosted, open-source fitness activity tracker and Strava alternative for logging workouts, gear, and health metrics.

long_form_blocks.heading: Overview
paragraphs: Endurain is a self-hosted fitness activity tracker built as an alternative to services like Strava, giving users full control over their workout data and where it is hosted. It imports activities from GPX, TCX, and FIT files and can integrate with Strava and Garmin Connect.
Built with a Vue.js/TypeScript frontend and a Python FastAPI backend on PostgreSQL, it is released under the AGPL-3.0+ license and deployed via Docker.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who It's For
paragraphs: Endurain suits cyclists, runners, and other athletes who want to own their activity data, plus households or small groups running a shared, multi-user fitness server instead of a commercial cloud platform.

features.title: Activity import
features.description: Import workouts from GPX, TCX, and FIT files.

features.title: Strava & Garmin Connect integration
features.description: Sync activities with existing Strava and Garmin accounts.

features.title: Gear tracking
features.description: Track components and gear such as bikes, shoes, and wetsuits.

features.title: Multi-user support
features.description: Admin-managed accounts for multiple users on one instance.

features.title: Health metrics logging
features.description: Log weight, steps, and sleep alongside workout data.

features.title: SSO and MFA
features.description: Single sign-on via OIDC/SAML and multi-factor authentication via TOTP.

pros: Full data ownership through self-hosting
Free under the AGPL-3.0+ open-source license
Imports from and syncs with Strava and Garmin Connect
Includes enterprise-style auth options (SSO, MFA) for a self-hosted project
cons: Requires Docker and self-hosting experience to deploy
Built and maintained largely by a single developer, so support is community-based
Commercial use of the Endurain trademark requires permission

faqs.question: Is Endurain free?
faqs.answer: Yes, it is free and open source under the AGPL-3.0+ license.

faqs.question: Does Endurain work with Strava or Garmin?
faqs.answer: Yes, it integrates with both Strava and Garmin Connect for syncing activities.

faqs.question: How is Endurain deployed?
faqs.answer: It is deployed via Docker on your own server, typically using the provided docker-compose examples.

faqs.question: Does Endurain support multiple users?
faqs.answer: Yes, a single instance supports multiple users with admin controls.

use_cases.title: Self-hosted Strava alternative
use_cases.description: Athletes who want to log and analyze workouts without a commercial cloud platform.
use_cases.audience: Cyclists, runners, and other endurance athletes

use_cases.title: Shared household fitness server
use_cases.description: Families or small groups running one multi-user instance for tracking activities together.

enrichment.seo_meta_description: Endurain is a free, open-source, self-hosted fitness tracker and Strava alternative for workouts, gear, and health metrics.

enrichment.short_description: Self-hosted, open-source outdoor activity tracker for logging workouts from GPX files with statistics, maps, and federation.

long_form_blocks.heading: Overview
paragraphs: FitTrackee is a self-hosted, open-source web application for tracking outdoor activities. Workouts can be created by uploading a GPX file (or a zip of several GPX files), or entered manually without a file, and are shown with maps and elevation/speed charts via OpenStreetMap.

It includes a statistics dashboard, equipment tracking, multi-user support with comments and follow requests, a documented REST API with OAuth 2.0, and optional ActivityPub-based federation. It is released under the AGPL license and remains under active development.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who It's For
paragraphs: FitTrackee is aimed at individuals, small clubs, and privacy-conscious athletes who want a self-hosted alternative to Strava or Garmin Connect, including those interested in federated, non-centralized activity sharing.

features.title: GPX-based workout tracking
features.description: Import GPX files (or a zip of files) to generate workouts with map and elevation/speed charts.

features.title: Manual workout entry
features.description: Log a workout by entering date, time, duration, and distance without a file.

features.title: Statistics dashboard
features.description: Track distance, duration, elevation gain, average speed, and pace over time.

features.title: Equipment tracking
features.description: Associate gear such as shoes or bikes with individual workouts.

features.title: REST API
features.description: Documented API with OAuth 2.0 for third-party integrations.

features.title: ActivityPub federation
features.description: Optional federation for sharing activity across instances without a central platform.

pros: Full control of workout data through self-hosting
Free under the AGPL open-source license
ActivityPub federation enables social features without a central server
Documented REST API for building integrations
cons: The project describes itself as under heavy development, so stability and features are still evolving
Smaller feature set than mature commercial activity trackers
Self-hosting requires setting up and maintaining a server

faqs.question: Is FitTrackee free?
faqs.answer: Yes, it is free and open source under the AGPL license.

faqs.question: Does FitTrackee support GPX file import?
faqs.answer: Yes, workouts can be created from GPX files or entered manually.

faqs.question: Does FitTrackee have an API?
faqs.answer: Yes, it provides a documented REST API with OAuth 2.0 authentication.

faqs.question: Can FitTrackee instances federate with each other?
faqs.answer: Yes, it supports optional ActivityPub-based federation.

use_cases.title: Private Strava alternative
use_cases.description: Runners and cyclists who want to keep workout data on their own server.

use_cases.title: Federated activity sharing
use_cases.description: Clubs or communities wanting to share activities across instances without a central provider.

enrichment.seo_meta_description: FitTrackee is a free, open-source, self-hosted outdoor activity tracker with GPX import, statistics, and ActivityPub federation.

enrichment.short_description: Free, open-source app that aggregates personal health records from patient portals like MyChart into one local, private view.

long_form_blocks.heading: Overview
paragraphs: Mere Medical is a free, open-source personal health record app that pulls medical records from multiple patient portals, including Epic MyChart, Cerner Health, Allscripts Connect, OnPatient, and FollowMyHealth, into a single unified view.

It connects to providers using the SMART on FHIR standard, stores data locally on the user's device rather than in the cloud by default, works offline, and requires no account registration to start. A self-hosted deployment option is also available for users who want to run their own instance.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who It's For
paragraphs: Mere Medical is built for patients whose medical history is spread across several healthcare systems, and for privacy-conscious individuals who want to keep their health records under their own control rather than in a hospital or vendor cloud.

features.title: Multi-portal aggregation
features.description: Connects to Epic MyChart, Cerner Health, Allscripts Connect, OnPatient, and FollowMyHealth.

features.title: Local data storage
features.description: Records are stored on the user's device rather than a third-party cloud by default.

features.title: SMART on FHIR standard
features.description: Uses HL7 SMART on FHIR protocols for secure, standards-based connections to provider portals.

features.title: Offline access
features.description: Full functionality is available without an internet connection once records are synced.

features.title: No registration required
features.description: Users can start using the app immediately without creating an account.

features.title: Self-hosting option
features.description: Can be deployed on a personal server for users who want to run their own instance.

pros: Health data stays local and private by default
No signup required to try it
Aggregates multiple patient portals into one place
Free and open source
cons: Only covers providers/portals that support SMART on FHIR or a supported connector
Each provider portal must be connected manually
Self-hosting requires technical setup

faqs.question: Is Mere Medical free?
faqs.answer: Yes, it is free and open source software.

faqs.question: Does Mere Medical store my data in the cloud?
faqs.answer: By default, data is stored locally on your device; a self-hosted option is also available.

faqs.question: Which patient portals does it support?
faqs.answer: It connects to portals such as Epic MyChart, Cerner Health, Allscripts Connect, OnPatient, and FollowMyHealth via SMART on FHIR.

faqs.question: Do I need to create an account?
faqs.answer: No, Mere Medical can be used without registering an account.

use_cases.title: Consolidating scattered medical records
use_cases.description: Patients who have seen multiple healthcare systems and want one unified record view.

use_cases.title: Private local health record keeping
use_cases.description: Individuals who want their medical history stored locally instead of in a vendor cloud.

enrichment.seo_meta_description: Mere Medical is a free, open-source app that aggregates health records from patient portals like MyChart into one private view.

enrichment.short_description: The most widely used open-source electronic health records and practice management system, free to self-host or run in the cloud.

long_form_blocks.heading: Overview
paragraphs: OpenEMR is described by its maintainers as the most popular open-source electronic health records (EHR) and medical practice management system. Its origins trace back to 2001, and it has been publicly available under the GNU General Public License since 2002. Version 8 achieved ONC Certification, confirming compliance with U.S. federal health IT standards.

The software is free to self-host on premises or in the cloud, and a free, fully hosted edition is also offered to U.S.-based healthcare providers through certified partners, alongside a network of professional support vendors for organizations that want paid implementation or hosting help.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who It's For
paragraphs: OpenEMR serves a wide range of healthcare settings, from independent practices and district hospitals to international NGOs such as the Peace Corps and IPPF, particularly organizations that need certified EHR functionality without ongoing software licensing fees.

features.title: Patient scheduling
features.description: Advanced scheduling with automated workflows and patient reminders.

features.title: Electronic prescribing
features.description: E-prescribing built into the clinical workflow.

features.title: Integrated medical billing
features.description: HIPAA ASC X12-compliant billing tools.

features.title: CMS reporting
features.description: Built-in reporting to support CMS program requirements.

features.title: Lab integration
features.description: Automated lab ordering and results processing.

features.title: Clinical decision support
features.description: A rules engine to support clinical decision-making.

features.title: Fine-grained access controls
features.description: HIPAA-compliant, role-based security and permissions.

features.title: Multi-language support
features.description: Interface support for over 30 languages.

pros: ONC-certified (version 8) for U.S. federal health IT compliance
Free to self-host with no software licensing cost
Free hosted edition available for U.S.-based providers via certified partners
Large global community plus a professional support vendor network
cons: Self-hosting requires HIPAA-aware server administration and security practices
Depth of features means a learning curve for new practices
The official free hosted edition is limited to U.S.-based providers

faqs.question: Is OpenEMR really free?
faqs.answer: Yes, it is free, open-source software under the GPL, self-hostable at no licensing cost, with a free hosted option for U.S.-based providers via certified partners.

faqs.question: Is OpenEMR ONC certified?
faqs.answer: Yes, version 8 achieved ONC Certification confirming compliance with U.S. federal health IT standards.

faqs.question: Can I get OpenEMR hosted for me?
faqs.answer: Yes, certified partners offer free hosting for U.S.-based providers, and a professional support vendor network offers paid hosting and support options.

faqs.question: Who supports OpenEMR?
faqs.answer: It's supported by a volunteer open-source community, community forums and chat channels, and a network of professional support vendors.

use_cases.title: Independent practice EHR
use_cases.description: Small and mid-size medical practices needing a certified EHR without licensing fees.

use_cases.title: Global health organizations
use_cases.description: NGOs and district hospitals needing a free, adaptable EHR across many languages and regions.

enrichment.seo_meta_description: OpenEMR is a free, open-source, ONC-certified electronic health records and practice management system, self-hosted or cloud.
